records.ety: [OF. project, F. projet, fr. L. projectus, p. p. of projicere to project; pro forward + jacere to throw. See Jet a shooting forth, and cf. Projet.]
usage: -- Project, Design. A project is something of a practical nature thrown out for consideration as to its being done. A design is a project when matured and settled, as a thing to be accomplished. An ingenious man has many projects, but, if governed by sound sense, will be slow in forming them into designs. See also Scheme.

defs: The place from which a thing projects, or starts forth.
That which is projected or designed; something intended or devised; a scheme; a design; a plan.
quotes: Vented much policy, and projects deep.
Projects of happiness devised by human reason.
He entered into the project with his customary ardor.

defs: An idle scheme; an impracticable design; as, a man given to projects.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Projected; p. pr. & vb. n. Projecting.]

defs: To throw or cast forward; to shoot forth.
quotes: Before his feet herself she did project.
Behold! th' ascending villas on my side Project long shadows o'er the crystal tide.

defs: To cast forward or revolve in the mind; to contrive; to devise; to scheme; as, to project a plan.
quotes: What sit then projecting peace and war?

defs: To draw or exhibit, as the form of anything; to delineate; as, to project a sphere, a map, an ellipse, and the like; -- sometimes with on, upon, into, etc.; as, to project a line or point upon a plane. See Projection, 4.
To shoot forward; to extend beyond something else; to be prominent; to jut; as, the cornice projects; branches project from the tree.
To form a project; to scheme.
